Event: Dean persistently makes unwanted romantic/sexual advances toward Jeff despite Jeff being straight and uncomfortable
Explanation: He gets some emotional gratification while repeatedly making Jeff uneasy and crossing his boundaries.

Event: Dean keeps and updates a ranked list of students and faculty he finds attractive
Explanation: He indulges his fantasies with little direct gain, while objectifying others and creating potential for discomfort and boundary violations.

Event: Dean escalates his attraction to Jeff into outright sexual harassment and stalking
Explanation: He gratifies his obsession but greatly increases potential harm and distress for Jeff.

Event: Dean habitually places his hand on Jeff's chest when exiting the study room
Explanation: He gets brief physical contact he desires while violating Jeff’s personal space.

Event: Dean crossdresses and wears stripper-like outfits as part of his costume habits
Explanation: He expresses himself and enjoys it; others are mostly neutral to mildly entertained, with no clear stated harm.

Event: Dean has a whole closet full of costumes in his office
Explanation: He invests in a hobby that gives him joy and supports events, slightly benefiting campus culture.

Event: Dean frequently drops into the study room in dumb costumes with irrelevant news
Explanation: He satisfies his need for attention and contact with the group while wasting their time and annoying them.

Event: Dean listens in on the study group’s conversations with a cup to the wall to know when to enter
Explanation: He gains information and engineered entrances at the cost of invading their privacy.

Event: Dean has a hard time resisting his lust for Jeff regardless of the situation and regularly lets it distract him
Explanation: His impulses undermine his professionalism and credibility while also making others uncomfortable with little real gain.

Event: Study group bans the Dean from attending Save Greendale Committee meetings
Explanation: They protect their efficiency and comfort by excluding him, at the cost of his feelings and possible administrative help.

Event: Dean buys and becomes addicted to an extremely lame virtual reality rig that makes him feel like a god
Explanation: He wastes time and likely resources while others are mostly unaffected or mildly inconvenienced.

Event: Dean uses blackmail and bribes to get the student body—especially Jeff and Annie—to do what he wants
Explanation: He successfully manipulates others to achieve his goals while restricting their autonomy and comfort.

Event: Dean is inexplicably catty toward Britta
Explanation: He vents and entertains himself through put-downs while harming his relationship with her.

Event: Dean openly dislikes Domingo and is not shy about letting him know during their fake publicity relationship
Explanation: He relieves his own frustration only slightly while damaging a cooperation that supports his public image.

Event: Dean helps instigate plots by stalking Jeff to get closer to him
Explanation: His obsession drives situations that bring him near Jeff at the expense of Jeff’s privacy and safety.

Event: Dean goes overboard trying to create a perfect commercial to promote Greendale in “Documentary Filmmaking: Redux”
Explanation: His overreach causes him stress and failure, providing only limited or dubious benefit to the school.

Event: Dean becomes distraught when he learns Greendale will be stripped down and rebranded as “Subway University”
Explanation: His deep emotional investment leaves him devastated while not changing the outcome.

Event: Dean’s incompetence in “Epidemiology” leads him to mistake hazardous experimental material for taco meat, causing a zombie outbreak
Explanation: He gets no benefit and endangers everyone with catastrophic, avoidable harm.

Event: Dean hires the unqualified Chang as Spanish teacher mainly out of fear of appearing racist
Explanation: He protects himself from imagined accusations but saddles the college with an incompetent teacher, undermining education.

Event: Dean’s actions in “Epidemiology” also help prevent a full Apocalypse How
Explanation: Despite earlier mistakes, he ends up contributing significantly to averting total catastrophe, benefiting all including himself.

Event: Dean creates the “Greendale Human Being” mascot out of political overcorrectness
Explanation: He advances his image as inclusive while imposing an awkward, confusing mascot that others find questionable.

Event: Dean creates the non-denominational “Mr. Winter” out of political overcorrectness
Explanation: He preserves his reputation for inclusivity while generating a contrived symbol that offers limited real benefit to others.

Event: Dean organizes college events obsessively because it’s the only way he can make Greendale and himself feel less like a failure
Explanation: He gains purpose and self-worth while improving campus life with events, even if motivated by insecurity.

Event: Dean wears ridiculous costumes to promote every school activity or event
Explanation: He enjoys performing and draws attention to activities, modestly benefiting participation and morale.

Event: Dean becomes more contemptuous of Britta as the seasons progress
Explanation: His growing disdain yields him little beyond momentary satisfaction while eroding cooperation and goodwill.

Event: Dean steps up as a competent paintball player and survives to the end with Jeff in “Modern Espionage”
Explanation: He improves his skills, helps win the game, and provides effective support to Jeff.

Event: Dean hides in a transparent closet and avoids labeling his complex sexuality until Season 6
Explanation: His refusal to be open causes him inner conflict, while sparing others from direct harm.

Event: School board pressures the Dean to label himself as exclusively gay as a publicity stunt to cover their homophobia
Explanation: They protect their image while causing him distress and misrepresenting his identity.

Event: Dean agrees to the board’s portrayal of him as a “Gay Dean” and does the publicity tour with Domingo
Explanation: He sacrifices his comfort and authentic identity to help the board’s PR and support Greendale’s image.
